mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and compresses credit spreads, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of FEDFUNDS levels remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9% signals severe labor market slack that suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 9%, or the mean return in those instances is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a coordinated demand recovery across manufacturing and consumer spending channels, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days that exceed the sum of individual effects.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both increasing month-over-month
failure_condition: Instances where INDPRO and RSAFS jointly rise fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of a systemic financial crisis, month-over-month changes in industrial production capture early inventory cycle restarts and capacity utilization adjustments, predicting positive S&P 500 direction over the next 5 trading days; this predictive power disappears once industrial capacity normalizes.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show Spearman correlation with absolute magnitude below 0.05 relative to 5-day forward returns outside of post-crisis industrial slack periods, or positive returns occur in fewer than 52% of rising-INDPRO instances.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation increases the probability of future monetary policy tightening and erodes real corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when CPIAUCSL is accelerating, or the Rank IC of CPIAUCSL momentum remains above -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in advance retail sales directly captures near-term consumer spending momentum, a dominant component of aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes exhibit a Rank IC below 0.03 with 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of rising-RSAFS months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment reduce recession probability and boost expected personal income growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive PAYEMS changes fail to produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases, or the mean forward return in those months is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero and consumer prices are decelerating, the joint condition signals monetary policy impotence combined with deflation risk that raises real debt burdens, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days; neither condition alone produces the effect.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with disinflation risk
failure_condition: The joint condition of FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and falling CPIAUCSL does not predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 2.0, or positive returns occur in more than 50% of joint-signal instances.
